title: The Patterson Procession
title_candidates: A New Name on the Trophy
theme: Two processions, settled early: Patterson seized the Trophy with the second-best round in TEG history and was never headed; Mullin took the Jacket from the 18th green of Round 1 and never gave it back. Everything else — Baker's bridesmaid grace, Williams's collapse, Neumann's second Spoon running — happened in the wake.
opening_hook: Open on John Patterson on the 1st tee at Praia D'El Rey, Saturday morning, a single point off the lead — then 49 points later, six hours older, walking off the 18th with the Trophy effectively in his pocket. Then rewind to a chaotic Friday at Royal Óbidos to explain how the field arrived there.
foreshadow: Patterson, Trophy maiden, starts R1 with a 10 at the SI 1 4th and is still only one off the lead by close of play — the field is that wild
Mullin arrives with six Jackets and back-to-back wins; the question is the margin, not the name
Williams is the defending Trophy champion — three wins in four TEGs, the man to beat
Neumann is the reigning Spoon holder, looking to shed it

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Maiden Trophy, won by 6 on 168 pts — the highest Trophy total in TEG history. Patterson sat second after a scrappy R1 at Royal Óbidos despite a sextuple-bogey 10 at the SI 1 4th. Then came R2 at Praia D'El Rey: 49 points, the second-best round ever played in a TEG, built on a 17-point burst across holes 9-13 and a 14-point closing stretch from 15. That single round opened a ten-shot lead he was never asked to defend. A wobbly R3 (three quadruples at Óbidos) trimmed it to three, but a calm 47 on Sunday saw him home by six over a fast-finishing Alex Baker.

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: Wire-to-wire, won by 10 at +67 — Mullin's seventh Jacket, third in a row. Outright lead from R1 H18 onwards, the moment he posted 87 to set a new Royal Óbidos course record (across six prior visits the previous best was 90). The cushion went 2 → 7 → 7 → 10 across the four rounds; Jon Baker, Jacket bridesmaid in three of the last four, settled for the silver again despite a personal-best Gross total of +77.

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Back-to-back Spoons. Bottom of the table from R1 H15 onwards — never out, never close to out. The decisive drop came on the par-3 15th at Royal Óbidos on day one, an 8 from which the climb never began. Two more rounds in the low 30s either side of a respectable 37 left him 41 points adrift of safety. Five quadruple-bogey-or-worse holes across the week did the bulk of the damage.

headline_candidates: Carnage at Óbidos: Mullin sets the record, Baker sets the other one
One Course, Two Records, Six Bruised Egos
rounds.chosen_headline: Carnage at Óbidos: Mullin sets the record, Baker sets the other one
rounds.angle: On Seve's final design, the two extremes of the day arrive in the same scorecard rack: Mullin breaks the Royal Óbidos course record at 87; Alex Baker establishes a new course-worst at 115. Around them, blow-ups everywhere — Patterson's 10 at the SI 1 4th, Williams's 9 at the 1st and 9 at the 15th, Neumann's pair of quintuples. Mullin in the Jacket lead by 2, Baker leads the Trophy by 1 over Patterson.

headline_candidates: Ten Clear Before the Halfway Bell
rounds.angle: The round the Trophy was decided. Patterson posts 49 points at Praia D'El Rey — the second-best round in TEG history — driven by a 17-point burst over five holes and a 14-point finish. From one off the lead to ten clear by the close. Alex Baker, on the same course, quietly posts his own personal-best 44 (Stableford) / +27 (Gross). Mullin extends to 7 in the Jacket; Williams posts a 10 at the 9th.

headline_candidates: Three Quadruples and Still in Front
rounds.angle: Royal Óbidos extracts its tax — Patterson signs for quadruples at the 12th, 15th-equivalent and 18th and a round of 34, slashing the Trophy lead from 10 to 3. But the chasers can't capitalise: Jon Baker's 16-point opening five at Óbidos hints at a charge that fades, and Mullin (despite his own 9 at the 10th) holds his 7-shot Jacket lead. Williams and Neumann both add to the blow-up ledger.

headline_candidates: Patterson Closes Out, Baker Climbs to Second
rounds.chosen_headline: Patterson Closes Out, Baker Climbs to Second
rounds.angle: Patterson's 47 closes the Trophy out by six; Alex Baker matches his earlier personal-best 44 (and +27 gross) to leapfrog into second — a remarkable arc from R1's course-worst to runner-up, with double-course PBs in his pocket. Mullin's 16 extends the Jacket margin to 10 despite a quadruple at the 1st; Williams adds an 11 at the 7th. Neumann's late 16-point burst (holes 11-15) is too late to dent the Spoon.

players.arc: Trophy maiden turned record-setter: a 10 at the SI 1 4th on Friday morning, a 49 on Saturday afternoon, a stagger through Sunday's Óbidos, a serene 47 to close. Wins by six on 168 — the highest Trophy total in TEG history — and a personal-best Gross total of +88 alongside it.

Seventh Jacket, third in a row, wire-to-wire from the moment he broke Royal Óbidos's course record on Friday. The 9 at the 10th in R3 and the 8 at the 1st in R4 were the only visible bruises on a fortnight of total Gross control.

The week's strangest arc: Royal Óbidos course-worst on Friday (115), then four days of steady improvement — personal-best 44s in both R2 and R4, double course PBs across the two venues, a personal-best Gross total of +127, and a leap to Trophy runner-up. The blow-ups (an 11 at the 14th, four quadruples) were still there; the rest of the golf finally outweighed them.

Bridesmaid again — Jacket runner-up for the fourth time in five TEGs, this time on a personal-best Gross total of +77 that on any other week would have won it. Briefly led the Trophy on R1 H6 and posted a 16-point opening burst in R3, but Mullin was simply unreachable and Patterson, by then, untouchable.

The defending Trophy champion's title defence never started. Three sextuple bogeys across the four rounds (9 at the 1st in R1, 10 at the 9th in R2, 11 at the 7th in R4) and a personal-best Gross total of +115 that says more about the previous visits than this one.

Back-to-back Spoons. Bottom from R1 H15 onwards, never closer than comfortable. A late 16-point Sunday burst from holes 11-15 hinted at the player who finished third in TEGs 5, 6 and 7, but the five quadruple-or-worse holes across the week told the truer story.

venue_notes: Third TEG visit to the Lisbon Coast, alternating the two Óbidos resorts — Seve Ballesteros's final design (Royal Óbidos, R1 & R3) and Cabell Robinson's coastal links-parkland hybrid (Praia D'El Rey, R2 & R4). Royal Óbidos in its third TEG visit produced both a course record (Mullin, 87) and a course-worst (Alex Baker, 115) on the same day. Praia D'El Rey in its fourth TEG visit hosted Patterson's 49. Lean on Seve's name once as colour in R1; mention the parkland/links split when Patterson's Praia masterpiece arrives in R2.
course_history_notes: Mullin's new Royal Óbidos personal best of 87 in R1 was simultaneously the new course record (prior best across six visits: 90).
Jon Baker's new Royal Óbidos personal best of 89 in R1 — 9 shots better than his last visit there.
Alex Baker double course-PB week: 102 at Royal Óbidos (R3, -6 vs last visit) and 100 at Praia D'El Rey (R2, -9 vs last visit), despite the R1 course-worst at Óbidos.
Neumann was 14 shots worse at Praia D'El Rey than his TEG 7 visit (R2 baseline) — the gap that sealed the Spoon.
Williams was 7 shots worse at Praia D'El Rey than his TEG 7 visit; +3 better at Óbidos but it didn't matter.
decisive_moments: Trophy: R2 H1 at Praia D'El Rey — Patterson takes outright lead and, by close of play, is 10 clear; the 49-point round (2nd-best in TEG history) is the moment the Trophy is effectively decided.
Green Jacket: R1 H18 at Royal Óbidos — Mullin closes out an 87 (new course record) to take outright Gross lead by 2; he leads by 2, 7, 7, 10 from there.
Wooden Spoon: R1 H15 at Royal Óbidos — Neumann's 8 at the par-3 15th drops him to last outright; he stays there for all 56 remaining holes.

payoffs.seed: Patterson the Trophy maiden, scrappy R1 with a 10 at SI 1
payoffs.payoff: The maiden answers with 49 points — the 2nd-best round in TEG history — and the Trophy chase is effectively over by Saturday teatime.

payoffs.seed: Mullin and the six Jackets — margin not name
payoffs.resolves_in: Round 1 and the Green Jacket summary
payoffs.payoff: Course record 87 on day one, lead never surrendered, wins Jacket #7 by 10.
Three sextuple bogeys (R1, R2, R4) and a never-in-it title defence — finishes well adrift.
Back-to-back Spoons confirmed; bottom from R1 H15 onwards, never threatened to escape.
